summary:
Dacomitinib has no documented role in treating glioblastoma; available evidence shows neither approval nor investigational status in CNS tumors. It lacks supportive data compared to established or studied alternatives like ERBB family inhibitors explored only peripherally. 

Based strictly on these findings, using dacomitinib would lack scientific justification in current guidelines.

Only two sentences needed.

Okay, final version meeting all requirements:

Dacomitinib is not indicated for glioblastoma due to absence of clinical trial or therapeutic evidence in brain tumor populations. The existing literature focuses on alternative TKIs in non-CNS settings rather than targeting EGFR pathways effectively in glial neoplasms.
